summary: [typescript] 14 tool files have TS2345 Init<Parameters,Metadata> type inference failures at Tool.define callsites. recommended_slice: Fix serializedWrite return type to align Promise<T> with internal Promise<void> chain; add installCli to ElectronAPI type or remove orphaned reference.

summary: [typescript] renderer/cli.ts calls window.api.installCli() which was removed from ElectronAPI type — causes typecheck failure in desktop package and a runtime crash if the install CLI feature is accessed.
recommended_slice: Remove or reconnect the installCli function in renderer/cli.ts to match the ElectronAPI type signature.

summary: Subagent telemetry roadmap captured: task_board fleet visibility, progress heartbeat streaming, auto-contention detection, consolidator protocol, session diff artifact, lessons register. Implementation requires runtime changes to task tool and coordination store.

Tool dispatch is inside AI SDK streamText, not opencode code — processor.ts only observes LLM events. AI SDK internally runs parallel tool calls via Promise.all. No concurrency control layer exists in the opencode tool execution bridge.
[typescript] TaskStatusChanged bus event defined in coord-events.ts but never published anywhere — task.ts uses DuckDB persistence + side-channel JSONL but skips Bus.publish entirely
[typescript] TaskClaim table and TS type lack lane_id, agent_role, and plan_id — cannot support fleet view needed for multi-lane orchestration tracking

Phase 2 refactoring extracted OAuth IPC handlers to github-ipc.ts and validation functions to ipc-validation.ts but never committed the new files, breaking desktop typecheck. Files now created — desktop typecheck restored.

Desktop Electron app decoupling audit complete — moderate score, key issues: magic-string IPC channels, GitHub OAuth secret in source, minimal test coverage (3 files), duplicated app identity, no centralized AppConfig

recommended_slice: Consolidate shared types (AgentDef, McpServerEntry, McpLocalConfig) into a shared package (e.g. @opencode-ai/types) that both app and desktop can import from

summary: [typescript] OpenCode desktop app UI audit: polished (not yet industry-leading). 48+ Kobalte-based accessible components, Oklch theme engine (38 themes), 70 editable keyboard shortcuts. Weaknesses: MessagePart 2414-line monolith, dual v1/v2 design system split, i18n parity gaps (zh.ts -89 keys). Full report at docs/json/opencode/uiux_audit_report.v1.json
BLOCKER: packages/desktop/src/main/github-ipc.ts imported but does not exist on disk. The file was removed from the import in ipc.ts but the new file was never created. Working tree is broken.

[typescript] prompt-input.tsx monolith (2171 lines) has 5 inline sub-components (ComposerPicker*, ComposerAgentControl, ComposerModelControl) and 6 natural extraction seams remaining. 14 sub-modules already extracted to ./prompt-input/. Single external consumer: session-composer-region.tsx.
[typescript] @opencode-ai/ui v1→v2 design system has 34 unmigrated v1 components, duplicate CSS layers with no cascade isolation, completely separate icon systems, and no barrel exports for v2

All 17 non-English locales in packages/app/src/i18n/ are missing 72-113 keys vs en.ts (963 total). dialog.agents (18 keys), dialog.mcp (19 keys), and github (13 keys) are entirely untranslated across ALL locales. Language coverage ranges from 88.2% (zht) to 92.5% (fr).

[typescript] The default port 4096 appears in all i18n locale files as a placeholder string for the server URL input field. These translatable strings should ideally reference a constant, but since i18n files are
recommended_slice: Future slice: i18n key for server URL placeholder should use template interpolation so the port comes from a single source. This would require changing both the i18n value and the code that renders the placeholder.

summary: 21/55 v1 components migrated to v2 (38%). 34 v1-only components remain un-migrated. No v2 barrel index exists. V2 CSS tokens use --v2- prefix, v1 tokens are unprefixed.

recommended_slice: After windows.ts is restored/git-checked-out, apply the two webContents.send replacements with IPC.push.* references

summary: IPC refactoring: 3 typecheck blockers — missing ipc-validation.ts module, preload/types.ts staged-deleted by concurrent session, getStore() type too narrow for IPC.store.* values

recommended_slice: Create coordination.ts in the tool directory or switch imports to use the correct @ alias path.

summary: Complete tool inventory: 74 files in packages/opencode/src/tool/ — 58 Tool.define-based tools, 16 helpers/utilities, 25 corresponding test files. 8 cacheable tools, 56 use Effect.orDie, all use Schema.Struct with .annotate() for parameters. Key services: AppFileSystem (most common), Ripgrep, LSP, Git, Storage, DuckDB.
OpenCode already has session viz infrastructure (activity feed + codebase map) unique among coding agents — the next leapfrog is a real-time agent thinking DAG visualizer that no competitor has built

recommended_slice: Create packages/desktop/src/main/tray.ts with Tray creation, icon management, badge counter, click-to-show-popover IPC, and context menu with quick actions

summary: Session processor lifecycle mapped: 2-layer loop (event-driven inner process + outer runLoop), implicit state machine via booleans, compaction is external fork, agent switching outside processor, background tasks fork separate sessions

[typescript] tool.ts is concurrently modified by Lane 0d (adding timeout/maxConcurrency/retry to Def interface) and by another lane (adding cacheable, R type param). The additive constraint means Lane 0d must care
recommended_slice: Read the current dirty state of tool.ts, verify exact current text, and use targeted search_replace with expected_before_sha256 to insert fields additively into the Def interface without touching existing dirty regions.

summary: [typescript] coordination.ts has been deleted from the working tree (git D) but is still imported by task.ts:15, write.ts:17, edit.ts:21, and registry.ts:42. Any build or typecheck will produce TS2307 errors for a
recommended_slice: Restore coordination.ts from git HEAD (git checkout dev -- packages/opencode/src/tool/coordination.ts) or remove all import sites and replace with the coord-events.ts mechanism if that was the intended replacement.

summary: ToolCache inflight Deferred never completed on compute defect — concurrent awaiters hang forever. Fix: use Deferred.into or Effect.exit+Deferred.done to propagate defects.

[typescript] Two blocker bugs (Deferred hang on compute failure, concurrent dedup race) and one major config bug (maxEntries=0) exist in the cache implementation. Under production concurrent load, compute failure
recommended_slice: Fix cache.ts: (1) add Effect.onError to fail the Deferred on compute failure, (2) use Ref.modify for atomic inflight dedup or switch to Effect.cachedWithTTL, (3) clamp maxEntries to minimum 1, (4) add config validation for NaN/negative values

summary: ToolCache: Deferred never failed on compute() defect — concurrent waiters hang forever. Inflight dedup check-then-set is non-atomic allowing duplicate computations.

[typescript] ToolCache is used by 9+ tools (read, grep, glob, read-artifact, read-source, read-lib, rig-schema-validate, rig-jsonl-query) but has zero test coverage. Two blocker bugs (Deferred orphan on compute fa
recommended_slice: Create packages/opencode/test/tool/cache.test.ts covering: inflight dedup atomicity, compute failure path (Deferred resolution on defect), concurrent access (fork-await pattern), expiry eviction boundary, LRU eviction at maxEntries limit, stats consistency across operations, invalidate/invalidateKey, maxEntries=0/1 edge cases, maxEntrySize bypass, negative/NaN config handling

summary: ToolCache: compute failure leaves Deferred pending — permanent deadlock for dedup'd fibers
ToolCache: Ref.update blind-assignment + Ref.set(new Map()) in invalidate can resurrect stale cache entries under concurrent load

recommended_slice: Consolidate all built-in lifecycle definitions into definition.ts, remove builtins.ts, update lifecycle.ts barrel to only re-export from definition.ts and engine.ts
